name: cairn
description: Portable agent environment bootstrap — memory, laws, skills, context templates. Works in Claude Code (cairn plugin), Antigravity, and Pi (pi.dev/@winnorton/cairn-pi). State lives in <project>/.cairn/; skills distributed as cairn-named packages. Domain-agnostic. Graduated adoption via tiers.

projectRoot.description: Root of the current work effort. The anchor for all cairn-state paths.

cairnRoot.description: Cairn-owned state directory — the only directory cairn writes to inside an adopter project. Everything cairn installs lives here: CLAUDE.md context, LAWS.md, memory tree, cairn-version marker. Git-tracked inside the adopter's repo. No agent-vendor config directory is ever a cairn write target.

files.description: Cairn context sections — the file the user's one import line (@./.cairn/CLAUDE.md) points at. The habitat is non-functional without it.
Top-level memory index — the lookup entry point for all memory
Meta-laws (the schema) + 7 seed laws. The shape is load-bearing; the seeds are scaffolding — replace with your own laws over time.
Reflective check-in — standardizes something capable agents do anyway
Plan-before-execute — standardizes something capable agents do anyway

Pre-emptive intent capture — file a quick thought as a dated note in the project repo. Distinct from /reflect (post-hoc) and from durable, agent-indexed cross-session memory. Includes the two-destinations disambiguation table.

External-perspective review of a change set by a fresh agent who didn't author it — reads diff PLUS adjacent unchanged files to catch inconsistency-class bugs the work-author missed. Distinct from /reflect (post-hoc same-agent). Named /peer-review to disambiguate from Claude Code's built-in /review skill.
Generate 2-4 alternative framings of the current problem — collaboration skill for rotating the solution space when convergent thinking is stuck
Structure cross-session context relay — collaboration skill for carrying output between agent sessions with minimal loss
Simulate end-user perspective before shipping — collaboration skill for rotating the observer position from builder to user
User memory conventions — always-on background, no citation
Feedback memory conventions — fires discretely, cite [MEM feedback/<name>]
Project memory conventions — decision-shaping, cite [MEM project/<name>]
Reference memory conventions — episodic lookups, cite [MEM reference/<name>]
lra researcher prompt — verbatim immutable master synced from the lra lab; the /lra skill copies it into a subject's research/PROMPT.md and fills placeholders. Never edit here (see PROVENANCE.md).
lra librarian prompt — verbatim master synced from the lra lab; the /lra skill copies it into a subject's library/PROMPT.md. Never edit here (see PROVENANCE.md).
lra per-subject operating rules (boundaries, source integrity, merge contract) — verbatim master synced from the lra lab; the /lra skill copies it into a subject's AGENTS.md.
lra prompt provenance — source commit + md5 fingerprints + the lab→cairn re-sync protocol. Records that the masters are verbatim and must not be edited in cairn.
How skills work in cairn — authoring guide for adding more
Post-install onboarding walk-through — grow out of it once the habitat is familiar
Stale memory/law review — becomes useful as the habitat accumulates entries
Citation frequency report — becomes useful once session history accumulates
File structured feedback to cairn's GitHub repo — cairn-ecosystem-specific (integrates with cairn's own issue tracker)

Artifact-creation skill: write a structured agent execution spec (docs/specs/) with phases, steps, checkpoints, executor handoff. Two lifecycles: execution specs (dispatch artifact, archived on ship) and contract specs (permanent source of truth — 3-pass verified status, spec updates before code). For non-trivial code changes that need an executor handoff. Distinct from /plan (behavioral, conversational) and from /note (single-paragraph intent capture).

Program-of-specs authoring: produce one master coordination doc + N workstream child stubs in docs/specs/ for substantial work that exceeds a single spec's scope. Bakes in no-deferral discipline, cross-cutting axes coverage (telemetry/diagnostics/rollback per workstream), informal axes-sign-off + commit-then-/peer-review at the master gate, and program-internal N-label bookkeeping for review-iteration traceability. Two invocation modes: cold-start and promote-from-spec (when a single /spec outgrew one file). Stubs are elaborated by `/spec --from`.

Round-review verb: trust-but-verify an executor's round of work against a /program-produced master, walk the master's Definition-of-Done section criterion-by-criterion against the diff (match sections by title, not §-number), surface zero-order findings outside the DoD, and draft (a) R+1 stub specs for each gap PLUS (b) an R+1 round master (SPEC_<NAME>_R<N>_00_MASTER.md) that's self-contained — execution order, round DoD, first-action checklist all inside the file so dispatch is a single one-liner (`/goal execute <round-master-path>`) with no caveats. Closes the orchestration loop: /program → /spec --from (elaborate) → executor → /round-review (produces round master + stubs) → next dispatch → repeat. Loop exits when /round-review writes zero stubs. Do NOT invoke if you authored the executor's implementation (anti-fresh-perspective).

Fast-execute verb — turn an autonomous executor agent (Google Antigravity Flash, Pi, or any harness with timer + file-read primitives) into a polling daemon that watches a sentinel-file inbox in docs/specs/, picks up dispatched specs (single specs OR /round-review-produced round masters — single specs are the more common shape), executes per the spec body (branches on structure — round masters use §4 first-action checklist, single specs use Pre-flight + Phases + Post-flight), marks completion via sentinel atomic-flip (.ready → .claimed → .done), and re-arms its timer. Default poll 300s; stops on explicit STOP.signal, 3 consecutive empty polls, malformed inbox, or a hard execution block; an unelaborated /program stub is NOT a stop — its sentinel flips .ready → .stub-rejected and polling continues (planning agent elaborates via /spec --from). Path-rejection guards on archive/ and _promoted/. Do NOT use in harnesses without timer primitives (fall back to manual /goal execute <path>) or for true one-shot dispatches where the polling-tail wait exceeds manual-prompt cost.

Prompt-evolve verb: extract a version-controlled operational prompt from a /spec for multi-pass work where each pass produces output and reusable insight. Coverage units may be known up front or discovered during early passes. The kernel requires inventory-before-write, explicit coverage/stop/re-run semantics, dependency-aware mutation, validation, an absolute self-edit target, a versioned CHANGELOG, and blocked-work capture; phase numbering and execution strategies remain adaptable. Stable mechanical rules graduate into schemas, validators, or tools. The artifact gets its own evolving lifecycle at `<project>/.cairn/prompt-evolve/<NAME>_PROMPT.md`; the source spec stays in place and receives an idempotent breadcrumb.

lra verb: a prompt-evolve specialization for researching a subject over many passes, run engine-free as project-local markdown an agent walks. Mirrors the lra research→library→application pipeline 1:1 through lra commands (subject create, collection create, research run, app serve), scaffolding a project-local research/ collection and driving two prompts (researcher + librarian) in native-file fallback mode — no MCP, SQLite, or server. The prompts are synced byte-for-byte from the lra lab (the researcher is immutable, ratchet- and bench-pinned there) into .cairn/context/lra/; the skill delivers and runs them, never edits them. lra is prompt-evolve's third worked instance (after fishing-agent and purduebb) and the source of the prompt-economy principle. Not in cairn-pi (outside its authoring-loop curation). See docs/CROSS_REPO_LRA_CAIRN.md.

Session-distill verb: read a past agent session JSONL transcript (Pi or Claude Code format, auto-detected) cold and produce a structured analysis of cairn-improvement findings — patterns recognized against a seeded catalog, skill candidates with 3-instance gate check, law candidates, memory candidates, environment-support gaps. This skill is the FORMALIZATION of cairn's foundational methodology — the transcript-analysis loop documented in docs/research/collaboration-skills.md and human-interaction-patterns.md, which produced /reframe, /bridge, /advocate, the collaboration-skills taxonomy, and most of cairn's existing skills. The methodology was running manually since v0.1; this skill makes it named and reproducible. Cousin to /peer-review (same observer-rotation mechanic; transcript instead of diff). Cousin to /reflect (which produces in-session distillate); /session-distill produces cross-session distillate for cairn-the-framework itself. Reports by default; never auto-applies changes. User-side `do it` gates any /note filing, skill edit, LAWS.md update, /feedback, etc. The skill body ships with a seeded catalog of patterns cairn has already named (cross-session relay → /bridge, reframing → /reframe, stub-spec-executed-anyway, status-without-spec-links, self-improving prompt, Pi-as-executor, etc.) so new findings are matched against history rather than re-invented. Promoted from docs/notes/_promoted/NOTE_CAIRN_INTROSPECT_SKILL_2026-04-26.md after the 3-instance gate was met in a single session (2026-06-09: 6+ instances).

Swarm verb: a living coordination surface for multi-workstream work that repeats over partitions and never finishes. Fan-out parallel stubs per wave, review results, self-improve the master, fan out the next wave. Use when work spans parallel workstreams AND iterates over partitions.

roles.essential: Load-bearing from day one. Habitat doesn't meaningfully function without this. Read first, fill in first.
roles.scaffolding: Grow-into territory. Shape is important (schemas, conventions, discoverability); specific content gets customized over time. Safe to skim at install, impossible to skip entirely.
roles.optional: Ergonomic win. Standardizes something a capable agent does anyway. Delete if you prefer less prompt surface; add back if consistency starts to slip.

tiers.description: Graduated adoption scope. Default install is 'full' (everything). Users who want a minimal, agent-portable habitat can request a lower tier via `adopt ... --tier <name>`. Each tier is cumulative — grow includes seed, structure includes grow, full includes structure.
tiers.seed: Agent-neutral core: CLAUDE.md + memory/MEMORY.md. Cross-session continuity, works with any agent (Claude, GPT, Gemini, local LLM) that can read markdown. The two-file minimum viable habitat.
tiers.grow: Adds durable conventions any careful agent can follow: LAWS.md schema, plan/reflect/note maintenance skills, the collaboration skills (reframe, bridge, advocate), the cross-perspective skills (peer-review). Still reasonably generic — content a non-Claude agent can use with a little orientation.
tiers.structure: Adds cairn's native hygiene loop: typed memory subdirectories, citation conventions, tour/prune/audit skills. Starts to assume cairn-aware tooling and conventions.
tiers.full: Adds cairn-ecosystem integrations like the feedback skill (files issues to cairn's own repo). Claude-Code/Cowork-optimized; default unless user requests a lower tier.

modes.create-if-absent: Write only if destination does not exist. Never overwrite.
modes.merge-append: Append cairn section to existing file (idempotent by marker).
modes.overwrite-with-backup: Back up existing to {dest}.bak, then overwrite. Requires explicit user approval.
modes.overwrite: Cairn-owned file that tracks upstream — replace on every (re-)adopt, no prompt and no backup. Use ONLY for files the user must not customize (e.g. the verbatim lab-synced lra prompt masters); never for user-editable scaffolding.

url_notes.https://cairn-feedback-591252228833.us-c: Cloud Run direct URL — DNS-independent, always-reliable.
url_notes.https://cairn.winnorton.com/feedback: Custom domain — may not resolve until CNAME propagates after initial setup. Kept as secondary because it's more memorable and survives Cloud Run service renames.

feedback.strategy: first-success: try URLs in array order; on network error, DNS failure, or 5xx, try the next URL before falling through to gh CLI and paste fallback
feedback.description: Primary delivery channel for the /feedback skill. Agents POST structured feedback JSON; endpoint creates a GitHub issue on winnorton/cairn and returns the issue URL. Three-level degradation: these endpoints, then `gh issue create`, then user-paste fallback.

payload_schema.category: gap | bug | skill-idea | clarification | docs | design
payload_schema.title: string, <=120 chars
payload_schema.environment: optional string (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai, etc.)
